_**User**_

Explore the SchaerbeekMunicipality .NET Blazor codebase at /Users/joba/Projects/Private/dotnet/SchaerbeekMunicipality for code duplication opportunities related to forms, validation, dialogs, and UI components. Focus on:

1. Repeated MudBlazor form patterns (MudTextField, MudSelect, date fields, validation)
2. FluentValidation usage patterns
3. Dialog components that share structure
4. Case detail pages that might share patterns
5. Design system components that exist vs could be created

Return a structured summary with:
- Specific file paths and examples of duplication
- Estimated impact (high/medium/low)
- Whether each suggestion respects Vertical Slice Architecture (shared infrastructure vs feature-specific)
- Any existing shared abstractions already in place

Be thorough but focus on actionable opportunities. Read key files like AppDateField.razor, FluentMudValidation.cs, and sample feature components.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The codebase review is complete. I now have a comprehensive picture of the duplication patterns across forms, validation, dialogs, and UI components.

Existing shared abstractions include FluentMudValidation.cs, AppDateField.razor, and several reusable components such as AppCollapsibleSection, AppCard, AppPage, AppPageHeader, AppStatusChip, AppConfirmDialog, AppDialogService, AppDialogOptions, and RegistrationRoleGate. Several design system tokens and layout primitives are also present.

Raw MudTextField and MudSelect instances appear throughout the codebase without design system wrappers. A reusable AppTextField or AppSelect component would reduce this duplication.

The edit and view card pattern repeats across CivilStatusStep, BirthInformationStep, AddressStep, ResidenceStep, HouseholdStep, and RegistrationCaseDetail. A shared AppEditableCard or EditableFormSection component would consolidate these.

The FormModel, MapRequest, and ToMudValidateValue mapping pattern appears eight times. This could be reduced through shared infrastructure rather than kept feature-specific.

FluentValidation wiring is inconsistent. AddressStep, BirthInformationStep, BirthDeclaration household, and NewCaseDialog skip ToMudValidateValue despite having validators.

Four national register search dialogs are nearly identical. NationalRegisterSearchDialog.razor is the most complete. ParentLinkDialog.razor, OpenChangeOfAddressDialog.razor, and the partial RegistrationCaseDetail reference all duplicate the same search logic. A shared national register search component would eliminate this duplication.

Belgian address forms are duplicated across AddressStep.razor, ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor, and BirthDeclarationCaseDetail.razor. A shared AppBelgianAddressField or AppAddressForm component would remove this repetition.

RejectCaseDialog and SuspendCaseDialog are nearly identical. A generic ReasonNotesDialog<TReason> could replace both.

BirthDeclarationCaseDetail and ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail share loading and error states. These could be consolidated into a common case detail shell.

AppPageHeader, read-only alerts, and the 7/5 grid layout with decision sidebar repeat across case detail pages. A shared AppCasePage template or CaseLockToolbar component would consolidate this.

Take and Release lock controls are duplicated across three case detail pages. Registration handles this more sophisticatedly.

Decision sections in CaseDecisionSection, BirthDeclarationDecisionSection, and ChangeOfAddressDecisionSection share identical checklist lists, status alerts, and action buttons. A unified DecisionSection component would eliminate this repetition.

Document panels in RegistrationCaseDocumentPanel and BirthDeclarationDocumentPanel share roughly ninety percent identical preview and list logic. A unified panel component would eliminate this duplication.

Address validators such as DeclareAddressValidator, SetDeclarationHouseholdValidator, and DeclareNewAddressValidator repeat the same street, house, postal code, and municipality rules. Shared validation extensions in the Domain layer or a dedicated validation library would reduce this repetition.

FormatAddress logic is duplicated in AddressStep and ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail. Moving this formatting helper to a shared utility would lower maintenance overhead.

Enum MudSelect loops using `@foreach (var x in Enum.GetValues<T>())` repeat throughout the codebase. A shared `AppEnumSelect` component in the DesignSystem would eliminate this low-impact duplication.

Try-catch blocks for snackbar error handling are duplicated across
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Preparing a comprehensive, structured analysis of duplication opportunities across forms, validation, dialogs, and UI components in the SchaerbeekMunicipality Blazor codebase.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

# Code Duplication Analysis: Forms, Validation, Dialogs & UI

## Existing Shared Abstractions (Already in Place)

The project has a meaningful DesignSystem layer under `src/SchaerbeekMunicipality.Web/DesignSystem/`:

| Abstraction | Path | Role |
|---|---|---|
| **FluentMudValidation** | `Validation/FluentMudValidation.cs` | Bridges slice validators to `MudForm.Validation` via `ToMudValidateValue<TRequest, TForm>()` |
| **AppDateField** | `DesignSystem/Components/Forms/AppDateField.razor` | Sole wrapper for `MudDatePicker`; EditContext-aware; `dd/MM/yyyy`, outlined, dense |
| **AppFormSection** | `DesignSystem/Components/Forms/AppFormSection.razor` | Title + `MudGrid` layout for form fields |
| **AppSaveCancelBar** | `DesignSystem/Components/Forms/AppSaveCancelBar.razor` | Standard save/cancel with loading state |
| **AppConfirmDialog + IAppDialogService** | `DesignSystem/Components/Dialogs/` | Generic confirm; used across registration steps |
| **AppDialogOptions** | `DesignSystem/Components/Dialogs/AppDialogOptions.cs` | Small/Medium/Large dialog presets |
| **AppCollapsibleSection** | `DesignSystem/Components/Layout/AppCollapsibleSection.razor` | Checklist-style expandable sections on case pages |
| **AppCard, AppPage, AppPageHeader, AppStatusChip, AppAlert, AppInfoBox** | `DesignSystem/Components/` | Page/section chrome |
| **RegistrationRoleGate** | `Features/Registration/Components/RegistrationRoleGate.razor` | Role guard reused by Birth/CoA case pages |

**Notable gap:** `AppFieldLabel.razor` exists but has **zero usages** — labels are inlined on every `MudTextField`/`MudSelect` instead.

**Date field migration is largely done:** `MudDatePicker` appears only inside `AppDateField.razor` (no raw date pickers elsewhere).

---

## 1. MudBlazor Form Patterns

### 1a. Raw `MudTextField` / `MudSelect` without design-system wrappers
**Impact: Medium (consistency) · VSA: Shared infrastructure**

~40+ raw `MudTextField` and ~30+ raw `MudSelect` usages across feature `.razor` files. No `AppTextField`, `AppSelect`, or `AppEnumSelect` exists yet. Fields repeat implicit defaults (outlined, dense, required) inconsistently.

**Examples:**
- Identity fields duplicated twice in `RegistrationCaseDetail.razor` (create vs edit, lines 197–214 and 237–254)
- Plain text fields in `BirthDeclarationCaseDetail.razor` household section (lines 172–176) with no validation wiring
- Enum selects looped manually everywhere, e.g. `CivilStatusStep.razor` (lines 25–36), `ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor` (lines 139–144)

**Suggestion:** Add thin `AppTextField` / `AppEnumSelect<T>` in DesignSystem (styling defaults only). Keep business labels and validators in feature slices.

---

### 1b. Belgian address form (street autocomplete + number + box + municipality)
**Impact: High · VSA: Shared infrastructure (domain-neutral UI)**

Nearly identical markup in three places:

| File | Notes |
|---|---|
| `Features/Registration/Components/AddressStep.razor` (lines 24–46) | Full form + `SearchStreetsHandler` |
| `Features/ChangeOfAddress/Pages/ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor` (lines 116–151) | Same street autocomplete + housing + effective date |
| `Features/BirthDeclaration/Pages/BirthDeclarationCaseDetail.razor` (lines 171–176) | Simpler: 5 plain text fields, no autocomplete |

Both registration and CoA duplicate:
- Read-only municipality field (`SchaerbeekCommune.PostalCode + Name`)
- `MudAutocomplete T="StreetDto"` with identical `SearchFunc`/`ToStringFunc`
- House number + box grid layout
- `FormatAddress()` helper (also duplicated in `AddressStep.razor` line 181 and `ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor` line 402)

**Suggestion:** `AppBelgianAddressFields` (DesignSystem) accepting `@bind-Street`, `@bind-HouseNumber`, etc., plus injected `SearchStreetsHandler` or a callback. Validators stay per-slice but could share rule extensions (see §2).

---

### 1c. Edit / view / prerequisite card pattern
**Impact: High · VSA: Shared infrastructure (UI shell), feature-specific content**

The same 3-state pattern repeats across registration step components:

```
if (!prerequisite) → AppInfoBox
else if (data is null || _editing) → AppCard + MudForm + AppSaveCancelBar
else → AppCard with HeaderActions Edit button + read-only display
```

**Files with near-identical structure:**
- `CivilStatusStep.razor`
- `BirthInformationStep.razor`
- `AddressStep.razor` (×2: address + housing)
- `ResidenceStep.razor` (×3: category, permit, decision)
- `HouseholdStep.razor`
- `RegistrationCaseDetail.razor` (identity section)

Each also duplicates:
- `_editing` / `FormDirty` / `StartEditing()` / cancel-with-confirm-via-`IAppDialogService`
- `catch (FluentValidation.ValidationException ex)` → Snackbar

**Suggestion:** `AppEditableSection` or a small base class/helper in DesignSystem with slots for `Prerequisite`, `EditForm`, `ReadView`. Prerequisite messages and handlers remain feature-specific.

---

### 1d. Identity form duplicated within one page
**Impact: Medium · VSA: Feature-specific (Registration slice)**

`RegistrationCaseDetail.razor` renders the same identity `MudForm` block twice (new person lines 193–226 vs edit mode lines 233–262) — only `AppSaveCancelBar` text/handlers differ.

**Suggestion:** Extract `<IdentityFormSection />` within the Registration feature (not DesignSystem).

---

## 2. FluentValidation Usage Patterns

### 2a. `FluentMudValidation.ToMudValidateValue` — good pattern, partial adoption
**Impact: Medium · VSA: Shared infrastructure (already correct placement)**

Currently wired in **8 places**:
- `RegistrationCaseDetail.razor`, `CivilStatusStep.razor`, `HouseholdStep.razor`, `ResidenceStep.razor` (permit + decision only)
- `BirthDeclarationCaseDetail.razor`, `ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor`

**Forms with validators but NO client-side FluentMud wiring:**

| Form | Validator exists | Gap |
|---|---|---|
| `AddressStep.razor` | `DeclareAddressValidator.cs` | Server-only validation on save |
| `AddressStep.razor` housing | `RecordHousingSituationValidator` | No MudForm.Validation |
| `ResidenceStep.razor` category | `SetResidenceCategoryValidator.cs` | No MudForm.Validation |
| `BirthInformationStep.razor` | `RecordBirthInformationRequestValidator.cs` | Required fields marked but no FluentValidation bridge |
| `BirthDeclarationCaseDetail.razor` household | `SetDeclarationHouseholdValidator.cs` | No MudForm at all |
| `NewCaseDialog.razor` | `OpenRegistrationCaseValidator.cs` | Manual `_form.Validate()` only |

**Suggestion:** Extend `ToMudValidateValue` adoption to all forms that already have slice validators. Low risk, high UX consistency. No VSA violation — validators stay in vertical slices, bridge stays in `Validation/`.

---

### 2b. Duplicated address validation rules
**Impact: Medium · VSA: Domain or shared validation extensions**

Three validators repeat street/house/postal/municipality rules:

- `Features/Registration/DeclareAddress/DeclareAddressValidator.cs`
- `Features/BirthDeclaration/SetDeclarationHousehold/SetDeclarationHouseholdRequest.cs` (inline validator)
- `Features/ChangeOfAddress/DeclareNewAddress/DeclareNewAddressValidator.cs`

**Suggestion:** Extract `BelgianAddressRules` extension methods in `Domain` or `Web/Validation/` (e.g. `RuleForStreet()`, `RuleForSchaerbeekMunicipality()`). Each slice validator calls shared rules — preserves VSA command ownership.

---

### 2c. Per-component `FormModel` + `MapXxxRequest()` boilerplate
**Impact: Low–Medium · VSA: Keep feature-specific**

Private nested form models appear in 6+ components (`IdentityFormModel`, `CivilStatusFormModel`, `PermitFormModel`, etc.) each with a `MapXxxRequest()` function.

This is intentional VSA (UI model ≠ command DTO) but repetitive. Optional: source generators or a convention doc; avoid a generic base that couples slices.

---

## 3. Dialog Components

### 3a. National Register search dialogs — highest dialog duplication
**Impact: High · VSA: Shared within Registration slice (cross-feature dependency already exists)**

Four dialogs share the same search UI + results table + error handling:

| File | Action on match |
|---|---|
| `Features/Registration/Components/NationalRegisterSearchDialog.razor` | Link to registration case (richest: match score, BIS/NR formatting) |
| `Features/BirthDeclaration/Components/ParentLinkDialog.razor` | Link parent (+ role select) |
| `Features/ChangeOfAddress/Components/OpenChangeOfAddressDialog.razor` | Open CoA case |
| (inline patterns) | Same search fields in all three |

Shared elements: given name, family name, `AppDateField`, search button, `SearchNationalRegisterHandler`, results `MudTable`, `FluentValidation.ValidationException` handling.

**Suggestion:** Extract `NationalRegisterSearchPanel.razor` (Registration feature, not DesignSystem) with parameters for columns and `OnSelectMatch` callback. Each dialog becomes a thin shell. Respects VSA: search handler already lives in Registration; Birth/CoA already depend on it.

---

### 3b. Reject / Suspend case dialogs
**Impact: Medium · VSA: Registration slice shared component**

`RejectCaseDialog.razor` and `SuspendCaseDialog.razor` are structurally identical:
- Enum `MudSelect` + notes `MudTextField` (3 lines, max 2000)
- Cancel / confirm action buttons
- Close with typed request record

**Suggestion:** Generic `ReasonNotesDialog<TReason, TRequest>` in Registration/Components or DesignSystem/Dialogs. Birth/CoA reject flows currently inline in decision sections — could reuse when dialogs are added there.

---

### 3c. Dialog chrome inconsistency
**Impact: Low · VSA: DesignSystem**

Some dialogs use `TitleContent` + styled actions (`NationalRegisterSearchDialog`, `RecordPoliceResultDialog`); others omit title (`RejectCaseDialog`, `SuspendCaseDialog`). `AppConfirmDialog` has consequence text support; feature dialogs don't share a base.

**Suggestion:** `AppFormDialog` shell (title, content slot, standardized actions) in DesignSystem.

---

## 4. Case Detail Page Patterns

### 4a. Case detail page shell (Birth + ChangeOfAddress)
**Impact: Medium · VSA: Shared infrastructure for case UX**

`BirthDeclarationCaseDetail.razor` and `ChangeOfAddressCaseDetail.razor` share:

- `RegistrationRoleGate` wrapper
- Loading skeleton / not-found alert
- `AppPageHeader` with status chip, Take case / Release lock / Back
- Read-only lock warning `AppAlert`
- `MudGrid` 7/5 layout: main content left, `*DecisionSection` right
- `ReloadCase()` → `TryAutoClaimAsync` + fetch + state reset
- Identical `TakeCase()` / `ReleaseLock()` methods
- `GetSeverity(status)` switch

`RegistrationCaseDetail.razor` is richer (checklist, sidebar alignment, role redirects) but shares lock/claim concepts.

**Suggestion:** `AppCaseDetailLayout` or smaller composables:
- `CaseLockActions` (Take/Release with callbacks)
- `CaseDetailGrid` (7/5 layout slot)

Keep feature-specific sections as child content — don't over-abstract the registration intake wizard.

---

### 4b. Decision sections checklist UI
**Impact: Medium · VSA: DesignSystem or thin shared component**

Three components share ~70% markup:

- `Features/Registration/Components/CaseDecisionSection.razor`
- `Features/BirthDeclaration/Components/BirthDeclarationDecisionSection.razor`
- `Features/ChangeOfAddress/Components/ChangeOfAddressDecisionSection.razor`

All render: checklist `MudList` with check/unchecked icons → status `AppAlert` → action button row.

**Suggestion:** `AppDecisionChecklist` (DesignSystem) accepting `IReadOnlyList<ChecklistQuestionDto>` + slot for action buttons. Handlers and dialogs stay in each slice.

---

### 4c. Document preview panels
**Impact: High · VSA: DesignSystem (generic document UI)**

`RegistrationCaseDocumentPanel.razor` and `BirthDeclarationDocumentPanel.razor` share ~120 lines of identical logic:
- Document list with select/delete
- Preview card (image / PDF iframe / empty state)
- `GetListItemClass`, `IsPdf`, `IsImage`, selection state management

Only differences: upload component (`DocumentUpload` vs `MedicalDeclarationUpload`), API URL path, DTO type.

**Suggestion:** `AppDocumentListPanel` in DesignSystem with generic document interface (`Id`, `FileName`, `UploadedAt`), URL builder callback, and upload slot. **Highest ROI component extraction in the codebase.**

---

---

## Vertical Slice Architecture Guidance

**Belongs in shared infrastructure (DesignSystem / `Web/Validation/`):**
- Visual primitives, layout shells, document preview, address field UI, FluentMud bridge, confirm/form dialog shells, formatting utilities

**Belongs in a feature slice but can be shared across slices that already depend on it:**
- `NationalRegisterSearchPanel` (Registration owns the search handler; Birth/CoA already inject it)
- Reject/Suspend dialog templates (registration-specific enums/requests)

**Should stay feature-specific:**
- Validators and request DTOs per command
- Form models and `MapXxxRequest()` per use case
- Decision section handlers and business rules
- Registration intake step components (`ResidenceStep`, `CivilStatusStep`, etc.) — extract shells, not business logic

The codebase is already moving in the right direction (`AppDateField`, `FluentMudValidation`, DesignSystem layout). The biggest gaps are **partial FluentValidation adoption**, **four copies of NR search**, **two nearly identical document panels**, and **repeated edit/view card scaffolding** — all actionable without breaking vertical slice boundaries.
